stories.name: 26-69-WB Website Hosting & Digital Marketing Services for the North Shore Workforce Board

events.summary: City of Salem Purchasing Department is soliciting open-market bids for website hosting and digital marketing services on behalf of the North Shore Workforce Board. The bid closing date was extended from June 16 to June 23, 2026 via Addendum 1. Bid documents (BD-23-1165-COSPD-COS01-130060) are available on CommBuys.

stories.name: 26-63-SPS Lease at Collins Middle School for Youth Outreach

events.summary: The City of Salem School Department is soliciting bids to lease space at Collins Middle School for a Youth Outreach Program. No square footage or lease value is stated. A pre-bid conference was held June 6, 2026; the RFP manual is available as an attachment on CommBuys.

stories.name: 26-64-SPS Lease at Salem High School for Child Care

events.summary: The City of Salem is soliciting proposals to lease space at Salem High School for a child care facility. This is an open-market, non-statewide solicitation (CommBuys BD-23-1165-COSPD-COS01-129370); a pre-bid conference was held June 6, 2026. Bid documents are available via the RFP manual attached to the CommBuys posting.

stories.name: 26-65-SPS Lease at Horace Mann Laboratory School for Early Education

events.summary: The City of Salem School Department is soliciting bids for a lease of space at Horace Mann Laboratory School to house an Early Education Program. A pre-bid conference was held June 6, 2026; bids are due June 16, 2026. Bid documents are available as '26-65-SPS RFP Manual - Lease at HMLS for ECC.pdf' via CommBuys (BD-23-1165-COSPS-COSPS-129373).

stories.name: 50 Washington Street — Tabernacle Congregational Church Renovation

events.summary: Continuance of a public hearing for renovation of the historic Tabernacle Congregational Church and construction of two additions within existing parking lots to create 48 studio apartments for veterans, while retaining the existing church uses. The project includes 9 parking spaces, utilities upgrades, and landscaping in the B5 Central Development District; applicant requested continuance to April 23, 2026.

Beacon Communities Services LLC proposes to renovate the Tabernacle Congregational Church building and add two new additions within existing parking lots to create 48 studio apartments for veterans at 50 Washington Street in the B5 (Central Development) district, while retaining existing church uses in the building. The Site Plan Review and Planned Unit Development Special Permit hearing was continued to May 21, 2026.

stories.name: Definitive Subdivision Plan of 15 Crosby Street

contacts.name: Scott Grover, Esq. f/b/o 15 Crosby LLC

events.summary: Continuance of a public hearing for a Definitive Subdivision Plan to split one existing lot at 15 Crosby Street into two lots, with a requested waiver of the Subdivision Control Law frontage requirement for Lot B.

Continued public hearing for a definitive subdivision to split one existing lot at 15 Crosby Street into two lots, with a requested waiver of frontage requirements for Lot B. Updated stormwater, grading, and sewer connection plans were presented; board cannot approve without complete contour plans, and the item was continued to April 23, 2026.

15 Crosby LLC received approval of a definitive subdivision plan to split one existing lot into two lots at 15 Crosby Street, including a waiver of frontage requirements for Lot B. Both the waivers and the decision passed 8-0 with one abstention, conditioned on updated contour and grade information being added to the civil plans prior to submission.

stories.name: 45 Jefferson Avenue Self-Storage Facility

contacts.name: Scott Grover, Esq. f/b/o ZXE, LLC

events.summary: Continuance of a public hearing for demolition of existing buildings and construction of a new four-story, approximately 100,000-square-foot self-storage facility with 735 storage units (721 interior, 14 exterior-access) in the Industrial, Coastal Resiliency Overlay, and Entrance Corridor Overlay Districts. Site improvements include 11 parking spaces, a two-way driveway, a two-lane drive aisle, and a stormwater management permit.

Continued public hearing for demolition of existing buildings at 45 Jefferson Avenue and construction of a new four-story, approximately 100,000-square-foot self-storage facility with 735 storage units (721 interior, 14 exterior) and 11 parking spaces in the Industrial, Coastal Resiliency Overlay, and Entrance Corridor Overlay Districts. Item was continued to April 23, 2026 at the applicant's request without substantive board discussion.

ZXE, LLC received site plan approval (9-0) to demolish existing buildings and construct a new four-story, approximately 100,000 sq ft self-storage facility with 735 units (721 interior, 14 exterior) at 45 Jefferson Avenue in the Industrial, Coastal Resiliency Overlay, and Entrance Corridor Overlay districts. The approved decision incorporates conditions from the City Engineering Department tied to demolition, building permit, and certificate of occupancy milestones, along with a Design Review Board positive recommendation, Waiver of Demolition Delay, and Order of Conditions already in hand.

stories.name: Forest River Residences Phase 1 (AvalonBay Communities)
stories.address: 8 Harrison Road, 9 Harrison Road, 262 Loring Avenue, 266 Loring Avenue, Salem, MA

contacts.name: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o AvalonBay Communities, Inc.
Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o WinnDevelopment Companies, LLC

events.summary: Continuance of a public hearing for Phase 1 of the Forest River Residences: a new 340-unit residential building with an above-grade parking structure (524 spaces), two enclosed outdoor courtyards, and an approximately half-acre central landscaped common in the Smart Growth and Coastal Resiliency Overlay Districts. The project also includes extension of Harrison Road and a pedestrian connection to the Forest River Conservation Trailhead.

Continuance of a public hearing for Phase 2 of the Forest River Residences: up to 145 dwelling units through adaptive reuse of three existing South Campus buildings (Harrington, Academic, and Alumni) plus construction of one new four-story residential building along Harrison Road, with no changes to existing building footprints. Site improvements include approximately 159 surface parking spaces and a network of landscaped common areas and pedestrian paths.

Continued joint public hearing before the Planning Board and Design Review Board for Smart Growth Overlay District Plan Review and C-ROD Site Plan Review. The applicant proposes up to 145 dwelling units through adaptive reuse of three existing Salem State University South Campus buildings and construction of one new four-story residential building along Harrison Road, with 159 surface parking spaces and landscaped common areas. This is Phase 2 of the Forest River Residences project.

Continued public hearing for Smart Growth Overlay District Plan Review and C-ROD Site Plan Review for Phase 1 of the Forest River Residences. The applicant proposes a 340-unit residential building with an above-grade parking garage (524 spaces), two enclosed outdoor courtyards, an approximately half-acre landscaped common, an extension of Harrison Road, and a connection to the Forest River Conservation Trailhead.

AvalonBay Communities received Smart Growth Overlay District plan approval (7-0, 2 abstentions) for Phase 1 of the Forest River Residences: a 340-unit residential building with an above-level parking garage (524 spaces), two outdoor courtyards, and an approximately half-acre landscaped common on former Salem State University South Campus parcels, including extension of Harrison Road and a connection to the Forest River Conservation Trailhead with five dedicated parking spaces.

WinnDevelopment Companies proposes up to 145 dwelling units through adaptive reuse of three former Salem State University South Campus buildings (Harrington, Academic, and Alumni) plus a new four-story residential building along Harrison Road, with 159 surface parking spaces and landscaped pedestrian paths — the second phase of the Forest River Residences. The Smart Growth Overlay District plan review hearing was continued to May 7, 2026.

stories.name: 4 Franklin Street Site Plan Amendment

events.summary: Public hearing for an amendment to a 2021 Site Plan Review, Flood Hazard Special Permit, and North River Canal Corridor Special Permit decision at 4 Franklin Street, reducing office space from two stories to one story and extending an ambulance garage. Updated building facade with simplified colors and trellis elements, HVAC relocated to rooftop, and revised landscaping and utilities were presented; engineering peer review is still pending and the item was continued to April 23, 2026.

CAS Salem, LLC seeks to amend a 2021 site plan approval at 4 Franklin Street in the North River Canal Corridor, reducing office space from two stories to one story and extending the ambulance garage, with revised parking layout, lighting, landscaping, and utilities; a second phase on the North River side is deferred. The hearing was continued to May 7, 2026.

stories.name: 170 North Street Site Plan Review

contacts.name: Jordan Ryan D/B/A Noble Horizon Homes LLC

events.summary: Public hearing for site plan review to legalize eight existing residential units at 170 North Street following a ZBA variance; the building has operated with eight units since 1999 despite being previously permitted for five, including a noncompliant basement unit. Proposed work includes adding an egress well to bring the basement unit into compliance and installing a new fire protection line; continued to April 23, 2026 pending engineering peer review and Design Review Board referral.

Noble Horizon Homes LLC proposes to create 8 residential units within an existing building at 170 North Street in the R2 and Entrance Corridor Overlay districts, with 16 parking spaces including an existing detached garage. The applicant did not appear and the site plan review hearing was continued to May 21, 2026.

stories.name: BlueWave Community Solar Program

events.summary: City Council authorized the Mayor to execute 20-year contracts with BlueWave, a Boston-based solar developer, to deliver energy savings from solar projects to income-eligible residential customers in Salem through the city's municipal aggregation program. A revolving fund may also be established to collect BlueWave incentive payments and reimburse the current aggregation supplier, First Point Power, for discounts it extends to eligible residents.

stories.name: 3A & 5 Winter Island Road ANR Endorsement
stories.address: 3A Winter Island Road / 5 Winter Island Road, Salem, MA

events.summary: Peter Haywood received 9-0 endorsement of an Approval Not Required (ANR) plan to incorporate land from an abutting parcel at 5 Winter Island Road into his existing yacht yard at 3A Winter Island Road, following the abutter's decision to sell. No new lots or structures are proposed.

stories.name: 262C Loring Ave & 8 Harrison Rd, Salem, MA
stories.address: 262C Loring Ave & 8 Harrison Rd, Salem, MA

contacts.name: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o AvalonBay Communities, Inc.

events.summary: Continued Conservation Commission public hearing on Notice of Intent (DEP #64-825) filed by AvalonBay Communities Inc for demolition of an existing building and construction of new multi-family residential buildings with associated infrastructure and stormwater improvements at the Salem State South Campus site (Map 31, Lots 1 & 327). Site is within a wetland-protected area under the Massachusetts Wetlands Protection Act and Salem's local ordinance.

Continued public hearing on a Notice of Intent for demolition of an existing building and construction of new multi-family residential buildings with associated infrastructure and stormwater improvements at the Salem State South Campus site (Map 31, Lots 1 & 327). No unit count or square footage is stated in this agenda.

stories.name: Lead Mills Seawall Repair

events.summary: Public hearing on a City of Salem Notice of Intent for in-kind seawall repairs and landside erosion restoration at the Lead Mills seawall on Lafayette St, a publicly owned coastal infrastructure site within a wetlands-protected area. No cost figures are stated.

stories.name: 25 Washington Square North Bed and Breakfast

events.summary: Petition continued to April 15, 2026 to operate a bed and breakfast at 25 Washington Square North. The applicants seek a parking variance (2 on-site spaces vs. 8 required, with 6 off-site spaces within a quarter mile) and a special permit for the B&B use; no exterior structural changes are proposed.

The ZBA approved a parking variance and special permit for Jeremy Schwartz and Shelby Soloff to operate a proposed six-guest-room, owner-occupied bed and breakfast at 25 Washington Square North. Two on-site parking spaces were approved where eight are required, subject to a special condition requiring documentation of at least three leased off-street spaces within a half mile during September–November 15. Interior work includes bathroom additions and removals, conversion of fireplaces to gas, and installation of sprinklers and fire suppression systems.

stories.name: 25 Bow Street / 32 Ord Street Lot Boundary Adjustment
stories.address: 25 Bow Street and 32 Ord Street, Salem, MA

events.summary: Petition continued to April 15, 2026 to adjust lot boundaries between 25 Bow Street and 32 Ord Street, yielding two nonconforming residential lots (5,008 sq ft and 6,909 sq ft) with deficient frontage, width, and lot area per dwelling unit. The petition also includes a special permit for a new second-story deck within the north setback of 25 Bow Street; an immediate abutter objected to the deck's privacy impact.

25 Bow Salem, LLC is seeking dimensional variance relief to reconfigure two nonconforming R1 lots—a 5,008 sq ft parcel on Bow Street and a 6,909 sq ft parcel on Ord Street (required minimum 15,000 sq ft)—along with a special permit for a second-story deck at 25 Bow Street within its north setback. A cease-and-desist was issued after the deck was constructed prior to ZBA approval; the board continued the petition to its May 6, 2026 special meeting for further review of Planning Board sequencing and abutter privacy concerns.

stories.name: 33-37 Osborne Street Auto Storage and Office Redevelopment

events.summary: Special permit petition continued to April 15, 2026 to replace a nonconforming heavy industrial use at 33-37 Osborne Street with a new two-story building featuring first-floor automobile storage and second-floor business offices. The proposal involves clearing the entire existing site and constructing a new structure that complies with dimensional and parking requirements.

The ZBA approved a special permit for Justin Cook to demolish an existing nonconforming heavy industrial structure on a half-acre site at 33-37 Osborne Street and construct a new two-story building for personal automobile storage (capacity 51 vehicles, with car elevator) and second-floor business offices, with 16 exterior parking spaces and modest landscaping. No vehicle repair will occur on-site; the historic commission previously waived a demolition delay for the existing structure, and further Planning Board approval is required.

stories.name: 102 Margin Street Rear Addition

events.summary: Continued petition at 102 Margin Street (R2 district) for Variances and a Special Permit to construct a rear addition and staircase encroaching into side and rear setbacks. Relief is also sought for lot area per dwelling unit (1,283 sq ft) and a parking reduction from 3 to 2 spaces, indicating a multi-unit residential property.

stories.name: 25 Bow Street and 32 Ord Street Lot Reconfiguration
stories.address: 25 Bow Street and 32 Ord Street, Salem, MA 01970

events.summary: Continued petition by 25 Bow Salem, LLC for Variance and Special Permit relief on two abutting R1 parcels with modified lot boundaries: a 5,008 sq ft Bow Street lot and a 6,909 sq ft Ord Street lot, both deficient in frontage, width, and lot area per dwelling unit. The Special Permit would authorize a new second-story deck within Bow Street's north setback.

stories.name: CarFive Motors 296 Highland Avenue Special Permit

events.summary: First hearing on a Special Permit for CarFive Motors LLC to add motor vehicle general repair and body work to an existing automobile dealership at 296 Highland Avenue in the B2/Entrance Corridor Overlay District. No structural changes to the building are described; the request addresses the use expansion only.

stories.name: 296 Highland Avenue, Salem, MA

events.summary: CarFive Motors LLC is seeking a special permit to add motor vehicle general repair and body work use to an existing automobile dealership at 296 Highland Avenue in the B2 / Entrance Corridor Overlay District. The matter was continued to the May 6, 2026 special meeting with no public comment received.

stories.name: Ordinance #46 — Home Occupation Zoning Amendment

events.summary: The Committee on Ordinances, Licenses and Legal Affairs discussed a draft zoning ordinance amendment that would expand allowable home occupations in Salem, permitting residents to operate small businesses (visual arts, recording studios, hair styling, family day care, etc.) from their homes without a special permit, subject to standards for appearance, activity, noise, parking, and storage. The committee debated enforceability questions — including square-footage caps (20% vs. 25% of gross floor area), shipment limits, accessory structures, and prohibited uses — but did not reach consensus on draft language. The item was kept in committee 5-0 pending a future meeting with city department heads including the building inspector and city solicitor.

stories.name: National Grid/Verizon Jointly Owned Pole Relocation – Cross Ave

events.summary: National Grid and Verizon's petition to relocate one jointly owned utility pole on Cross Avenue was continued to the April 9, 2026 City Council meeting at the applicant's request in order to first meet with the Ward Councillor.

The City Council approved relocation of one jointly owned National Grid/Verizon utility pole on Cross Avenue, closing a continued public hearing. The approval was conditioned on the relocated pole carrying secondary transmission lines only — no primary lines or transformers — and included an informal request to the Tree Commission to replace a mature cherry tree displaced by the work.

stories.name: Forest River Residences Phase 2 (WinnDevelopment)
stories.address: 8 Harrison Road, 9 Harrison Road, 262 Loring Ave, and 266 Loring Ave, Salem, MA

contacts.name: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o WinnDevelopment Companies, LLC
Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o AvalonBay Communities, Inc.

events.summary: Joint public hearing closed 8-0 by both the Planning Board and Design Review Board for Phase 2 of the Forest River Residences on the former Salem State University South Campus. WinnDevelopment proposes up to 145 senior housing units through adaptive reuse of three historic campus buildings (Harrington, Academic, and Alumni) and construction of one new four-story building along Harrison Road, with 159 surface parking spaces. The project will receive historic tax credits and is coordinated with the adjacent AvalonBay Phase 1; no final approval vote was taken at this session.

Continued public hearing for Phase 1 of the Forest River Residences, with AvalonBay proposing a 340-unit residential building, a 524-space above-grade parking garage, and a half-acre central landscaped common on the former Salem State University campus. Updated plans address 2070 flood elevation analysis, accessible pathway grading, raised crosswalk treatments, and building signage; continued to April 23, 2026.

stories.name: Haunted Warren Museum

events.summary: The City Council approved extended operating hours for the Haunted Warren Museum, a proposed ticketed indoor paranormal-education experience at 259 Essex Street, on a 90-day trial basis commencing upon issuance of a certificate of occupancy. Approved hours are 6 AM–10 PM Sunday through Thursday and 6 AM–midnight Friday and Saturday, with a follow-up hearing scheduled for September 10, 2026 to reassess.

stories.name: National Grid Conduit Installation – Lafayette St & Savoy Rd
stories.address: Intersection of Lafayette St and Savoy Rd, Salem, MA (service at 354 Lafayette St)

events.summary: National Grid received City Council approval to install underground conduits at the intersection of Lafayette Street and Savoy Road to provide new electrical service to Salem State University at 354 Lafayette Street.

stories.name: National Grid/Verizon Jointly Owned Pole Installation – Lafayette St & Fairview Rd
stories.address: Intersection of Lafayette St and Fairview Rd, Salem, MA

events.summary: National Grid and Verizon received City Council approval to install one jointly owned utility pole at the intersection of Lafayette Street and Fairview Road to support underground electrical work at Salem State University.

stories.name: Mansell Field Remediation & Restoration Project – Underdrain

events.summary: The City Council appropriated $60,000 from the Capital Outlay account for installation of an underdrain to complete the Mansell Field Remediation & Restoration Project, a public park that has been fenced off since 2020 due to flooding and overgrowth. The project returns the park to public use.

stories.name: 14-16 Bridge Street Lot Split

events.summary: ZBA approved a variance to re-split a merged lot at 14-16 Bridge Street into two nonconforming lots: a 2,982-sq-ft lot at 14 Bridge Street (41.25-ft frontage) and a 4,094-sq-ft lot at 16 Bridge Street (50-ft frontage), both below the 60-ft minimum. The petitioner plans to construct a new two-unit residence on the newly created lot; additional Planning Board relief will be required before construction.

stories.name: 99 Leach Street 3.5-Story Two-Family House

events.summary: ZBA denied a variance to construct a 3.5-story two-family house on a nonconforming lot in the Coastal Resiliency Overlay District. The proposal sought relief for lot area per dwelling unit (2,222 sq ft vs. 7,500 required), building height (3.5 vs. 2.5 stories), and rear setback (23 ft vs. 30 ft required); the motion failed 3-2, with opponents arguing self-created hardship, flood risk, and dimensional inconsistencies in the submitted plans.

stories.name: 74 Beaver Street Lot Split and Two-Family Townhouse

events.summary: ZBA approved a variance to subdivide 74 Beaver Street into two nonconforming lots — a 4,502-sq-ft parcel along Beaver Street and a 5,621-sq-ft parcel along Silver Street — and to construct a new three-bedroom two-family townhouse with a 3-ft front setback and 12-ft rear setback. A condition requires a revised plot plan showing three code-compliant parking spaces before a building permit is issued.

stories.name: 102 Margin Street Two-Family Conversion

events.summary: Petition continued to April 15, 2026 to legalize a de-facto two-family use at 102 Margin Street by adding a rear exterior staircase and converting an existing rear porch to a bedroom, with no footprint increase. The Board requested dimensioned plans with parking layout, a statement of hardship, and clarification on the full extent of zoning relief needed, including potential parking relief.

events.project_name: 26-69-WB Website Hosting & Digital Marketing Services for the North Shore Workforce Board
events.summary: City of Salem Purchasing Department is soliciting open-market bids for website hosting and digital marketing services on behalf of the North Shore Workforce Board. The bid closing date was extended from June 16 to June 23, 2026 via Addendum 1. Bid documents (BD-23-1165-COSPD-COS01-130060) are available on CommBuys.

events.project_name: 26-65-SPS Lease at Horace Mann Laboratory School for Early Education
events.summary: The City of Salem School Department is soliciting bids for a lease of space at Horace Mann Laboratory School to house an Early Education Program. A pre-bid conference was held June 6, 2026; bids are due June 16, 2026. Bid documents are available as '26-65-SPS RFP Manual - Lease at HMLS for ECC.pdf' via CommBuys (BD-23-1165-COSPS-COSPS-129373).

events.project_name: 26-64-SPS Lease at Salem High School for Child Care
events.summary: The City of Salem is soliciting proposals to lease space at Salem High School for a child care facility. This is an open-market, non-statewide solicitation (CommBuys BD-23-1165-COSPD-COS01-129370); a pre-bid conference was held June 6, 2026. Bid documents are available via the RFP manual attached to the CommBuys posting.

events.project_name: 26-63-SPS Lease at Collins Middle School for Youth Outreach
events.summary: The City of Salem School Department is soliciting bids to lease space at Collins Middle School for a Youth Outreach Program. No square footage or lease value is stated. A pre-bid conference was held June 6, 2026; the RFP manual is available as an attachment on CommBuys.

events.address: 3A Winter Island Road / 5 Winter Island Road, Salem, MA
events.summary: Peter Haywood received 9-0 endorsement of an Approval Not Required (ANR) plan to incorporate land from an abutting parcel at 5 Winter Island Road into his existing yacht yard at 3A Winter Island Road, following the abutter's decision to sell. No new lots or structures are proposed.

events.applicant: Jordan Ryan D/B/A Noble Horizon Homes LLC

The applicant did not appear and the site plan review hearing was continued to May 21, 2026.

Both the waivers and the decision passed 8-0 with one abstention, conditioned on updated contour and grade information being added to the civil plans prior to submission.

events.address: 8 Harrison Road, 9 Harrison Road, 262 Loring Ave, 266 Loring Ave, Salem, MA
events.summary: Continued public hearing for Smart Growth Overlay District Plan Review and C-ROD Site Plan Review for Phase 1 of the Forest River Residences. The applicant proposes a 340-unit residential building with an above-grade parking garage (524 spaces), two enclosed outdoor courtyards, an approximately half-acre landscaped common, an extension of Harrison Road, and a connection to the Forest River Conservation Trailhead.

contacts.name: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o AvalonBay Communities, Inc.

events.address: 8 Harrison Road, 9 Harrison Road, 262 Loring Ave, and 266 Loring Ave, Salem, MA
events.summary: Joint public hearing closed 8-0 by both the Planning Board and Design Review Board for Phase 2 of the Forest River Residences on the former Salem State University South Campus. WinnDevelopment proposes up to 145 senior housing units through adaptive reuse of three historic campus buildings (Harrington, Academic, and Alumni) and construction of one new four-story building along Harrison Road, with 159 surface parking spaces. The project will receive historic tax credits and is coordinated with the adjacent AvalonBay Phase 1; no final approval vote was taken at this session.

contacts.name: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o WinnDevelopment Companies, LLC

events.summary: Continued public hearing for a definitive subdivision to split one existing lot at 15 Crosby Street into two lots, with a requested waiver of frontage requirements for Lot B. Updated stormwater, grading, and sewer connection plans were presented; board cannot approve without complete contour plans, and the item was continued to April 23, 2026.

contacts.name: Scott Grover, Esq. f/b/o 15 Crosby LLC

events.summary: Continued public hearing for demolition of existing buildings at 45 Jefferson Avenue and construction of a new four-story, approximately 100,000-square-foot self-storage facility with 735 storage units (721 interior, 14 exterior) and 11 parking spaces in the Industrial, Coastal Resiliency Overlay, and Entrance Corridor Overlay Districts. Item was continued to April 23, 2026 at the applicant's request without substantive board discussion.

contacts.name: Scott Grover, Esq. f/b/o ZXE, LLC

events.address: 8 Harrison Road, 9 Harrison Road, 262 Loring Ave, and 266 Loring Ave, Salem, MA
events.summary: Continued public hearing for Phase 1 of the Forest River Residences, with AvalonBay proposing a 340-unit residential building, a 524-space above-grade parking garage, and a half-acre central landscaped common on the former Salem State University campus. Updated plans address 2070 flood elevation analysis, accessible pathway grading, raised crosswalk treatments, and building signage; continued to April 23, 2026.

contacts.name: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o AvalonBay Communities, Inc.

events.summary: Public hearing for an amendment to a 2021 Site Plan Review, Flood Hazard Special Permit, and North River Canal Corridor Special Permit decision at 4 Franklin Street, reducing office space from two stories to one story and extending an ambulance garage. Updated building facade with simplified colors and trellis elements, HVAC relocated to rooftop, and revised landscaping and utilities were presented; engineering peer review is still pending and the item was continued to April 23, 2026.

events.applicant: Jordan Ryan D/B/A Noble Horizon Homes LLC
events.summary: Public hearing for site plan review to legalize eight existing residential units at 170 North Street following a ZBA variance; the building has operated with eight units since 1999 despite being previously permitted for five, including a noncompliant basement unit. Proposed work includes adding an egress well to bring the basement unit into compliance and installing a new fire protection line; continued to April 23, 2026 pending engineering peer review and Design Review Board referral.

contacts.name: Jordan Ryan D/B/A Noble Horizon Homes LLC

events.summary: The City Council approved relocation of one jointly owned National Grid/Verizon utility pole on Cross Avenue, closing a continued public hearing. The approval was conditioned on the relocated pole carrying secondary transmission lines only — no primary lines or transformers — and included an informal request to the Tree Commission to replace a mature cherry tree displaced by the work.

events.project_name: Haunted Warren Museum Extended Hours Petition
events.summary: The City Council approved extended operating hours for the Haunted Warren Museum, a proposed ticketed indoor paranormal-education experience at 259 Essex Street, on a 90-day trial basis commencing upon issuance of a certificate of occupancy. Approved hours are 6 AM–10 PM Sunday through Thursday and 6 AM–midnight Friday and Saturday, with a follow-up hearing scheduled for September 10, 2026 to reassess.

events.project_name: National Grid Conduit Installation – Lafayette St. & Savoy Rd.
events.address: Intersection of Lafayette St. and Savoy Rd., Salem, MA
events.summary: National Grid received City Council approval to install underground conduits at the intersection of Lafayette Street and Savoy Road to provide new electrical service to Salem State University at 354 Lafayette Street.

events.project_name: National Grid & Verizon Joint Pole Installation – Lafayette St. & Fairview Rd.
events.address: Intersection of Lafayette St. and Fairview Rd., Salem, MA
events.summary: National Grid and Verizon received City Council approval to install one jointly owned utility pole at the intersection of Lafayette Street and Fairview Road to support underground electrical work at Salem State University.

events.project_name: National Grid & Verizon Joint Pole Relocation – Cross Ave.
events.summary: National Grid and Verizon's petition to relocate one jointly owned utility pole on Cross Avenue was continued to the April 9, 2026 City Council meeting at the applicant's request in order to first meet with the Ward Councillor.

The City Council appropriated $60,000 from the Capital Outlay account for installation of an underdrain to complete the Mansell Field Remediation & Restoration Project, a public park that has been fenced off since 2020 due to flooding and overgrowth. The project returns the park to public use.

events.project_name: 50 Washington Street — Tabernacle Congregational Church Renovation
events.summary: Continuance of a public hearing for renovation of the historic Tabernacle Congregational Church and construction of two additions within existing parking lots to create 48 studio apartments for veterans, while retaining the existing church uses. The project includes 9 parking spaces, utilities upgrades, and landscaping in the B5 Central Development District; applicant requested continuance to April 23, 2026.

events.project_name: Definitive Subdivision Plan of 15 Crosby Street
events.applicant: Scott Grover, Esq. f/b/o 15 Crosby LLC
events.summary: Continuance of a public hearing for a Definitive Subdivision Plan to split one existing lot at 15 Crosby Street into two lots, with a requested waiver of the Subdivision Control Law frontage requirement for Lot B.

contacts.name: Scott Grover, Esq. f/b/o 15 Crosby LLC

events.applicant: Scott Grover, Esq. f/b/o ZXE, LLC
events.summary: Continuance of a public hearing for demolition of existing buildings and construction of a new four-story, approximately 100,000-square-foot self-storage facility with 735 storage units (721 interior, 14 exterior-access) in the Industrial, Coastal Resiliency Overlay, and Entrance Corridor Overlay Districts. Site improvements include 11 parking spaces, a two-way driveway, a two-lane drive aisle, and a stormwater management permit.

contacts.name: Scott Grover, Esq. f/b/o ZXE, LLC

events.address: 8 Harrison Road, 9 Harrison Road, 262 Loring Avenue, 266 Loring Avenue, Salem, MA
events.applicant: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o AvalonBay Communities, Inc.
events.summary: Continuance of a public hearing for Phase 1 of the Forest River Residences: a new 340-unit residential building with an above-grade parking structure (524 spaces), two enclosed outdoor courtyards, and an approximately half-acre central landscaped common in the Smart Growth and Coastal Resiliency Overlay Districts. The project also includes extension of Harrison Road and a pedestrian connection to the Forest River Conservation Trailhead.

contacts.name: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o AvalonBay Communities, Inc.

events.address: 8 Harrison Road, 9 Harrison Road, 262 Loring Avenue, 266 Loring Avenue, Salem, MA
events.applicant: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o WinnDevelopment Companies, LLC
events.summary: Continuance of a public hearing for Phase 2 of the Forest River Residences: up to 145 dwelling units through adaptive reuse of three existing South Campus buildings (Harrington, Academic, and Alumni) plus construction of one new four-story residential building along Harrison Road, with no changes to existing building footprints. Site improvements include approximately 159 surface parking spaces and a network of landscaped common areas and pedestrian paths.

contacts.name: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o WinnDevelopment Companies, LLC

events.summary: ZBA approved a variance to re-split a merged lot at 14-16 Bridge Street into two nonconforming lots: a 2,982-sq-ft lot at 14 Bridge Street (41.25-ft frontage) and a 4,094-sq-ft lot at 16 Bridge Street (50-ft frontage), both below the 60-ft minimum. The petitioner plans to construct a new two-unit residence on the newly created lot; additional Planning Board relief will be required before construction.

ZBA denied a variance to construct a 3.5-story two-family house on a nonconforming lot in the Coastal Resiliency Overlay District. The proposal sought relief for lot area per dwelling unit (2,222 sq ft vs. 7,500 required), building height (3.5 vs. 2.5 stories), and rear setback (23 ft vs. 30 ft required); the motion failed 3-2, with opponents arguing self-created hardship, flood risk, and dimensional inconsistencies in the submitted plans.

events.project_name: 74 Beaver Street Lot Split and Two-Family Townhouse
events.summary: ZBA approved a variance to subdivide 74 Beaver Street into two nonconforming lots — a 4,502-sq-ft parcel along Beaver Street and a 5,621-sq-ft parcel along Silver Street — and to construct a new three-bedroom two-family townhouse with a 3-ft front setback and 12-ft rear setback. A condition requires a revised plot plan showing three code-compliant parking spaces before a building permit is issued.

events.project_name: 25 Washington Square North Bed and Breakfast
events.summary: Petition continued to April 15, 2026 to operate a bed and breakfast at 25 Washington Square North. The applicants seek a parking variance (2 on-site spaces vs. 8 required, with 6 off-site spaces within a quarter mile) and a special permit for the B&B use; no exterior structural changes are proposed.

Petition continued to April 15, 2026 to legalize a de-facto two-family use at 102 Margin Street by adding a rear exterior staircase and converting an existing rear porch to a bedroom, with no footprint increase. The Board requested dimensioned plans with parking layout, a statement of hardship, and clarification on the full extent of zoning relief needed, including potential parking relief.

events.project_name: 25 Bow Street / 32 Ord Street Lot Boundary Adjustment
events.address: 25 Bow Street and 32 Ord Street, Salem, MA
events.summary: Petition continued to April 15, 2026 to adjust lot boundaries between 25 Bow Street and 32 Ord Street, yielding two nonconforming residential lots (5,008 sq ft and 6,909 sq ft) with deficient frontage, width, and lot area per dwelling unit. The petition also includes a special permit for a new second-story deck within the north setback of 25 Bow Street; an immediate abutter objected to the deck's privacy impact.

events.project_name: 33-37 Osborne Street Auto Storage and Office Redevelopment
events.summary: Special permit petition continued to April 15, 2026 to replace a nonconforming heavy industrial use at 33-37 Osborne Street with a new two-story building featuring first-floor automobile storage and second-floor business offices. The proposal involves clearing the entire existing site and constructing a new structure that complies with dimensional and parking requirements.

events.address: 262C Loring Ave & 8 Harrison Rd, Salem, MA
events.summary: Continued Conservation Commission public hearing on Notice of Intent (DEP #64-825) filed by AvalonBay Communities Inc for demolition of an existing building and construction of new multi-family residential buildings with associated infrastructure and stormwater improvements at the Salem State South Campus site (Map 31, Lots 1 & 327). Site is within a wetland-protected area under the Massachusetts Wetlands Protection Act and Salem's local ordinance.

contacts.name: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o AvalonBay Communities, Inc.

entities.name: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o AvalonBay Communities, Inc.
Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o WinnDevelopment Companies, LLC

upcoming.project_name: 26-69-WB Website Hosting & Digital Marketing Services for the North Shore Workforce Board
upcoming.summary: City of Salem Purchasing Department is soliciting open-market bids for website hosting and digital marketing services on behalf of the North Shore Workforce Board. The bid closing date was extended from June 16 to June 23, 2026 via Addendum 1. Bid documents (BD-23-1165-COSPD-COS01-130060) are available on CommBuys.

upcoming.project_name: 26-65-SPS Lease at Horace Mann Laboratory School for Early Education
upcoming.summary: The City of Salem School Department is soliciting bids for a lease of space at Horace Mann Laboratory School to house an Early Education Program. A pre-bid conference was held June 6, 2026; bids are due June 16, 2026. Bid documents are available as '26-65-SPS RFP Manual - Lease at HMLS for ECC.pdf' via CommBuys (BD-23-1165-COSPS-COSPS-129373).

upcoming.project_name: 26-64-SPS Lease at Salem High School for Child Care
upcoming.summary: The City of Salem is soliciting proposals to lease space at Salem High School for a child care facility. This is an open-market, non-statewide solicitation (CommBuys BD-23-1165-COSPD-COS01-129370); a pre-bid conference was held June 6, 2026. Bid documents are available via the RFP manual attached to the CommBuys posting.

upcoming.project_name: 26-63-SPS Lease at Collins Middle School for Youth Outreach
upcoming.summary: The City of Salem School Department is soliciting bids to lease space at Collins Middle School for a Youth Outreach Program. No square footage or lease value is stated. A pre-bid conference was held June 6, 2026; the RFP manual is available as an attachment on CommBuys.
